$schema_note: Referent guard: a divine-Name / Prophet / proper-noun sense must NEVER be carried onto a common word that merely shares its letters or root. An attribute glosses by who it points to. When the referent forces a non-divine / non-proper reading, emit the referent-correct common gloss; when the referent is unidentified, emit pending (reason referent_unresolved or proper_name). PENDING beats a wrong gloss. Read names with norm_strict/bare/QAC POS, never the recall-only norm().
generator: authored by hand; aligned to nahw/examples/ayah-context-decisions.jsonl and function-word-decisions.jsonl
principle: An attribute or homograph is glossed by its REFERENT. A human/created referent forbids a divine-Name reading; a common-noun referent forbids a Prophet/proper-noun reading.

rules.referent_test: who is the described entity in this āyah?

decision_table.when_referent: a human (e.g. Ibrāhīm in إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ)
decision_table.reason: referent is a human; حَلِيمٌ is a human attribute here, NOT a divine Name
referent is Allah; render as the divine attribute
do not assume a divine Name until the referent is fixed

rules.hard_guard: never default an adjective like حَلِيم/كَرِيم/عَزِيز of a human to 'one of Allah's Names'

rules.referent_test: is this a name of the Prophet, or the common adjective/maṣdar?

decision_table.reason: naṣb common adjective qualifying a deed; NOT the proper name of the Prophet Ṣāliḥ
definite proper-noun referent; render the name
do not carry the Prophet sense onto the common adjective without a proper-noun referent

rules.hard_guard: صَٰلِحًا as object of عَمِلَ is the common adjective; the Prophet reading requires a proper-noun referent

rules.surface_class: proper / descriptive names sharing a verbal root
rules.reason: a proper noun is never glossed as its homographic verb; render the name or pending(proper_name)
rules.guard: POS mismatch: no 'to …' verb gloss on a proper noun

general_guards: Divine Names are render-by-referent: an attribute of a created being is the common adjective, not a Name.
Prophet/proper names are never their homographic common verb/noun (مُحَمَّد ≠ 'to praise'; صَٰلِحًا as a deed ≠ the Prophet).
Prefer pending(referent_unresolved | proper_name) over a referent-wrong gloss.
